etymology.description: phonetic 辛 (chisel) + semantic 見 (see) -> stylized as 亲 (variant of 辛) -> 親
etymology.interpretation: 親 (qīn, 'kiss') combines 辛 (xīn, 'chisel') phonetically with 見 (jiàn, 'see') semantically. The character suggests a close action that involves attentiveness, like seeing. The association with kissing likely comes from the intimate gesture of 'coming close to see'.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 親 is simplified to 亲 by replacing 見 with the simpler component 见.

examples.chinese: 他轻轻地亲了她的额头(他輕輕地親了她的額頭)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de qīn le tā de étóu.

examples.chinese: 分手之后(分手之後)，他们没有再联系(他們沒有再聯繫)，也没有再亲吻过(也沒有再親吻過)。
examples.pinyin: Fēnshǒu zhīhòu tāmen méiyǒu zài liánxì, yě méiyǒu zài qīnwěn guò.
examples.english: After the breakup, they didn't contact each other again nor did they kiss anymore.

examples.chinese: 他习惯在睡前亲她的脸颊(他習慣在睡前親她的臉頰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xíguàn zài shuì qián qīn tā de liǎnjiá.
examples.english: He got used to kissing her cheek before sleep.
